title: Vijay under pressure, DMK-AIADMK scramble to stop TVK from forming govt: TN chaos in 10 points
content: Tamil Nadu's political landscape shifted dramatically as Vijay's TVK emerged as the largest party, winning 108 seats but falling short of a majority. Intense negotiations followed, with Congress backing TVK, causing friction with the DMK. Accusations of gubernatorial pressure surfaced, while both TVK and AIADMK reportedly engaged in resort politics amid a complex government formation battle.

title: Horrific murder on CCTV: Woman chased, stabbed to death by husband in Telangana
content: A shocking incident unfolded in Suryapet district as a 24-year-old housewife was brutally stabbed to death by her estranged husband at a tea stall. The victim, Sirisha, had left her abusive husband, Manideep, over marital disputes. Despite police intervention for counselling, Manideep ambushed and fatally attacked her in public, leading to her death.

title: BCCI warns IPL teams over honey-trap risks, tightens hotel room access rules
content: The BCCI has issued a stern warning to IPL franchises regarding 'targeted compromise and honey-trapping,' enhancing security and access protocols. Stricter rules now govern hotel room access, unauthorised departures, and accreditation display. Franchise owners face new restrictions on interacting with players during matches, and vaping is strictly prohibited, with severe penalties for violations.

title: Elon Musk tells Anthropic: We will cancel SpaceX deal if your AI tools cause harm to humanity
content: SpaceX has partnered with AI rival Anthropic, providing significant computing resources for its Claude AI software. This move surprised many given Elon Musk's past criticisms of Anthropic. Musk stated he leased computing power after ensuring Claude is "good for humanity," reserving the right to reclaim it if the AI causes harm.

title: Rs 27 crore, one big question - What has happened to Rishabh Pant in T20s?
content: Rishabh Pant’s unbeaten 32 helped Lucknow Super Giants beat Royal Challengers Bengaluru by nine runs in Lucknow, but questions over his T20 form continue to grow. Once seen as one of India’s brightest white-ball stars, Pant has struggled for consistency in recent IPL seasons despite becoming the league’s most expensive player at Rs 27 crore. His falling returns in T20 cricket, changing role at LSG and growing competition for India spots have added to the pressure, with Irfan Pathan also admitting the wicketkeeper-batter is under scrutiny.

title: Nvidia share falls to zero in China; CEO says latest chips shouldn’t reach Beijing
content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang declared America a priority for advanced chip sales, stating China should not have access to the "latest and greatest" AI technology. However, he urged the US not to abandon the Chinese market entirely, arguing that global sales and increased tax revenues strengthen American economic and national security.

title: Who will govern Tamil Nadu? TVK MLAs threaten mass resignation; AIADMK MLAs holed up in resort
content: Tamil Nadu's political landscape is in flux as Vijay's TVK emerged as the largest party but fell short of a majority. Governor Rajendra Vishwanath Arlekar has sought proof of support, sparking criticism from TVK allies who demand a floor test. Meanwhile, DMK and AIADMK are reportedly exploring an alliance to counter TVK's surge.

title: Harassed in relationship, Class 12 student dies by suicide in Gurgaon
content: A 17-year-old student tragically ended her life in Gurgaon, reportedly due to harassment in a suspected relationship. Her suicide note details feelings of being misunderstood and disrespected by a youth she knew for a year, along with an alleged incident at tuition. Police are investigating, having contacted the boy mentioned and retrieved chats.

title: Eye on future: Shreyas Iyer likely to replace Suryakumar Yadav as India T20I captain
content: Indian selectors are set to remove Suryakumar Yadav as India's T20I captain when the squads for the Ireland and England series are picked. Due to his batting struggles, the selectors have run out of patience with the 35-year-old. Shreyas Iyer is a strong contender to take over the leadership role as the team looks for a fresh start ahead of a packed T20I calendar.

title: 'You have 10 working days to voluntarily recognise unions': Google UK staff tells company
content: Google's DeepMind employees are demanding union recognition amid concerns over their AI technology's use by the US and Israeli militaries. Workers seek a commitment against developing AI weapons and surveillance tools, urging management to voluntarily recognize their unions or face legal action. The move highlights growing anxieties about AI's ethical implications and its potential for harm.

title: ISI-linked module busted: Delhi temple, military camp, UP police stations were on target list
content: Pakistan's ISI allegedly plotted terror attacks in Delhi and Haryana, targeting a historic temple, a popular highway dhaba, and a military camp. Operatives linked to the Shahzad Bhatti module conducted reconnaissance, sharing photos and videos with handlers. The plan aimed to cause panic, disruption, and mass casualties, with police stations in Uttar Pradesh also on the radar.

title: Railway cop tied to pole, lynched by mob in police presence for rape bid
content: A railway constable was lynched by a mob on Thursday in Bhubaneswar after allegedly attempting to rape a woman. The incident, captured on video, saw the deceased tied to a pole before being declared dead at the hospital. Police have arrested four individuals and are identifying others involved.

title: One year after Operation Sindoor, how India is still keeping Pakistan under pressure
content: India's Operation Sindoor, though its kinetic phase has ended, continues to exert pressure on Pakistan. Following successful strikes on terror infrastructure and air defenses, India remains vigilant, ready to retaliate against any future provocations. Beyond military action, diplomatic and economic measures are actively impacting Islamabad, ensuring Pakistan remains militarily unbalanced and countering its narrative.

title: Oil at $101 but could Strait of Hormuz crisis push prices to $200?
content: Escalating Middle East conflict has choked the world's busiest oil passage, pushing crude prices past $100 per barrel with warnings of a surge to $167 or higher. A prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz threatens a severe energy and trade shock, potentially reducing global trade growth significantly and deepening economic uncertainty worldwide.

title: Vinesh Phogat cleared WADA’s retirement rule, but Asiad dream looks out of bounds now
content: Wrestler Vinesh Phogat has reportedly met WADA's six-month notice requirement for returning athletes, according to sources. Despite this, her comeback is facing scrutiny over registration issues and a whereabouts failure. Her eligibility for upcoming events, including the Asian Games, remains uncertain due to these controversies and selection policies.

title: 'Vomited major toxin’: How rat poison inside watermelon led to death of 4 in Mumbai
content: Rat poison, specifically zinc phosphide, has been identified as the cause of death for four members of the Dokadia family in Mumbai. Forensic tests confirmed the poison was inside the watermelon they consumed. Police are now investigating whether the incident was accidental, suicidal, or homicidal, examining all possibilities.

title: Rupee in red: Currency falls 45 paise to 94.67 against US dollar
content: The Indian rupee opened sharply lower against the US dollar, falling 36 paise to 94.58 due to rising Middle East tensions. This marked a reversal from Thursday's gains, as US President Trump warned Iran of stronger military action.

title: Watch: Muslim jail officer marries ex-murder convict; 'kanyadaan' performed by Bajrang Dal leader
content: An assistant jail superintendent and a former life convict, who met while he was serving his sentence at Satna Central Jail, have married. Their love story, which began with official interactions, culminated in a Hindu wedding ceremony in Chhatarpur district, becoming a local sensation.

title: ‘Line ke bahar daal …’: Pant’s on-field masterplan removes Tim David — Watch
content: Rishabh Pant's sharp tactical advice proved pivotal in Lucknow Super Giants' narrow nine-run DLS victory over Royal Challengers Bengaluru. Caught on camera instructing spinner Shahbaz Ahmed, Pant's guidance led to the crucial dismissal of the dangerous Tim David. This breakthrough shifted momentum, ultimately helping LSG end their losing streak and revive playoff aspirations.

title: First time in 1110 days! Virat Kohli falls for duck as Prince Yadav stuns RCB
content: Prince Yadav's stunning 140.4 kph nip-backer, inspired by Virat Kohli's own advice, shattered the RCB star's stumps, marking Kohli's first IPL duck in over three years. This pivotal moment, coupled with Mitchell Marsh's century, propelled Lucknow Super Giants to a nine-run DLS victory over Royal Challengers Bengaluru, keeping LSG's playoff dreams alive.

title: Over 100 DMK-appointed Tamil Nadu law officers resign amid uncertainty over TVK govt formation
content: Over 100 law officers representing the DMK government and its agencies have resigned amid uncertainty over the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam forming the new government.  They have been asked to continue for a week until new appointments are made, as the state cannot be left unrepresented.  Typically, the incoming government appoints its legal representatives shortly after taking office.

title: DMK to prop up ADMK govt in TN? Stalin will take call; EPS likely to meet guv with claim today
content: The legislators left the decision to Stalin, multiple sources told TOI. AIADMK general secretary Edappadi K Palaniswami, meanwhile, is understood to have sought governor R V Arlekar’s appointment on Friday.

title: Bengaluru road rage: IAF officer, wife allege assault by auto driver
content: An Indian Air Force officer and his wife were allegedly assaulted in a road rage incident near Jalahalli Cross after requesting an auto driver to make way. The altercation escalated, with the officer reportedly being attacked with a tree branch and both individuals being chased and hit by a group of men.

title: 50 seconds, 8 bullets: Cops hunt 4 hired killers after Suvendu aide’s murder
content: Siliguri police detained a vehicle owner after his car's registration number was linked to the murder of Suvendu Adhikari's personal assistant. Investigators found the number plate was fake, and the owner had advertised the car for sale online. Meanwhile, a seized motorbike also had a fake number plate and erased chassis number, complicating the hunt for its owner.

title: Oil prices jump as US-Iran tensions intensify; Brent crude stays beyond $100, WTI up 3%
content: Oil prices surged Friday as US-Iran tensions flared, reversing earlier declines.  US strikes on Iranian targets prompted accusations of ceasefire violations, escalating fears over the vital Strait of Hormuz.  This geopolitical volatility overshadows concerns about suspicious trading activity linked to policy announcements, impacting global energy supplies and inflation.

title: ‘Virat bhaiya only told me’: Prince Yadav stuns Kohli using RCB star’s own advice — Watch
content: Lucknow Super Giants secured a vital nine-run DLS victory over Royal Challengers Bengaluru, keeping playoff hopes alive. Mitchell Marsh's explosive 111 powered LSG to 209/3, while Prince Yadav's fiery 3/33, including the remarkable wicket of Virat Kohli off his own advice, sealed the contest. RCB's chase faltered despite Patidar's 61.

title: Top stocks to buy: Stock recommendations for today - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: NLC India, and Sammaan Capital have been chosen by Bajaj Broking Research as the top stocks to buy on May 8, 2026, with a 1-month target horizon.

title: IPL playoff qualification scenarios: RCB still in control despite Lucknow setback
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru's playoff hopes remain strong despite a recent loss, holding a solid third place with 12 points. While their Net Run Rate offers a crucial advantage, top-order batting instability is a growing concern. One more win could secure their spot, but avoiding a late collapse against tough upcoming opponents is vital for a smooth qualification. RCB still look like a strong playoff contender, but unless they rediscover consistency quickly, the final stretch of the league phase could become far tighter than it currently appears.

title: Kyle Loftis death sparks questions months after major crash
content: Kyle Loftis, founder of 1320Video, died Tuesday night, according to the platform’s official statement. His cause of death has not been confirmed yet. Loftis helped build one of the biggest motorsports media brands online through street racing and automotive content. Fans, racers, and creators across social media continue sharing tributes and remembering his influence on modern car culture.

title: Simone Biles open up on therapy as Jonathan Owens eyes NFL return
content: Simone Biles opens up about her mental health struggles following the Tokyo Olympics, reflecting on the pressure that led her to step away from competition. In a recent interview, the Olympic champion shared how the experience changed her perspective on strength and vulnerability. Two years later, Biles continues to focus on her well-being while keeping her future in gymnastics open, including a possible return for the 2028 Olympics in Los Angeles.

title: Joe Senser dies at 69 as unanswered questions surround final chapter of his life
content: Former Minnesota Vikings tight end Joe Senser has passed away at 69. Senser, a Pro Bowler and the only Vikings tight end to achieve over 1,000 receiving yards in a season, died on May 7th. The cause of death remains unknown, though he had been dealing with the aftermath of a severe stroke suffered in 2016.

title: Big setback for Trump: US court rules 10% global tariffs unlawful
content: A US federal trade court ruled against President Trump's 10% global tariffs, deeming them invalid and unauthorized by law. The court found the administration's justification under a 1974 trade law insufficient, a setback for a key trade policy. This decision offers stability to businesses reliant on global supply chains.

title: Raja Shivaji Day 7 box office: Film crosses Rs 60 crore in week 1
content: Riteish Deshmukh's 'Raja Shivaji' has concluded its first week with impressive box office numbers, crossing the Rs 60 crore gross mark. Despite initial audience skepticism, Deshmukh's portrayal of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj has garnered widespread acclaim. The historical drama, which he also wrote and directed, continues to draw crowds, with its Marathi version performing particularly strongly.

title: 'Not all players are happy': Ex-India cricketer drops explosive claim on Gambhir
content: Former India cricketer Atul Wassan has raised concerns about the Indian team's dressing room atmosphere under head coach Gautam Gambhir. Wassan suggests that not all players are comfortable with Gambhir's intense and uncompromising style. He hinted that some cricketers feel sidelined despite the team's recent successes. This comes amid significant changes in the Indian squad, including retirements and captaincy shifts.

title: 'AIADMK Must Leave BJP': DMK Sources Say 'Can't Tie-Up With Communal Party'
content: Vijay's TVK won 108 seats in the April election but fell 10 short of majority, the scenario leading to chaos in Tamil Nadu as the actor tries to rally the extra numbers while his rivals - the DMK and AIADMK - plot a historic alliance to keep him out

title: Over 1 Lakh Schools Lack Power, 98,500 Without Girls' Toilet: Niti Aayog Report
content: Niti Aayog Report: As many as 98,592 schools in the country do not have functional girls' toilets while 61,540 don't have any usable toilet.

title: US-Iran War News LIVE Updates: UAE Reports New Strikes As US-Iran Ceasefire Hangs By Thread
content: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: A US admiral said US forces sank six small Iranian ships. Iran denied any had been sunk and earlier fired warning shots at US warships

title: Ex-China Defence Ministers Get Suspended Death Sentences In Corruption Cases
content: Chinese President Xi Jinping's anti-corruption drive has resulted in the removal of China's highest ranking general and military leadership.

title: How Saudi's Defiance Pushed Trump To Halt 'Project Freedom' In Hormuz
content: Saudi Arabia is frustrated with the American commander-in-chief's "erratic handling of the war", due to which Middle Eastern nations, especially the United Arab Emirates (UAE), have borne the brunt of Iran's retaliatory strikes.

title: Telangana Engineering Student Stabbed 15 Times, Allegedly By Lover's Brother
content: According to locals, nearly 10 people arrived on motorcycles, confronted Yuvan and entered into an argument before stabbing him 15 times with knives

title: Tamil Nadu Government Formation LIVE:  Cannot Tie-Up With Any “Communal Party": DMK Asks AIADMK To Dump BJP
content: Tamil Nadu Election Results 2026 Live Updates: There is buzz that the Dravidian arch-rivals DMK and AIADMK may come together after over 50 years to form a government and keep Vijay out of power.

title: Life Insurance vs Fixed Deposit: The Winner On Returns May Surprise You
content: Over long horizons, equity exposure and compounding can deliver returns that beat traditional fixed deposits -- especially after tax.

title: Royal Family Seeks Action On Sabarimala Issues From New Kerala Government
content: PN Narayana Varma, representative of the Pandalam royal family, demanded that cases registered against Sabarimala devotees during the protests be withdrawn without delay.

title: "Gundagardi": Mother Of Suvendu Adhikari's Aide Blames Trinamool For Murder
content: Calling for justice, Chandranath Rath's mother demanded life imprisonment for the gunmen over death sentence.

title: Mamata Banerjee Stays Defiant, Assembly Dissolved: What Are Her Options Now?
content: As Mamata Banerjee continues to claim wrong doing in Assembly Elections, it has been anticipated the legal battle over Bengal might escalate to courts.

title: All 107 TVK MLAs To Resign If DMK, AIADMK Try To Form Government: Sources
content: The TVK, which won 108 seats -- two of them won by Vijay -- has 107 MLAs and argues that as the single-largest party, it should be invited by the Governor to form government.

title: Trump Calls Hormuz Strikes Exchange 'Love Tap', Iran's "Lion" Warning
content: The skirmishes raised concerns over the viability of a shaky ceasefire that had largely held for the previous month. But Donald Trump insisted it remained intact despite new violations, which he described as a "love tap".

title: Google Faces New UK Lawsuit Over Online Advertising Practices
content: The claim filed on behalf of British advertisers is seeking up to £3 billion ($4 billion) in compensation, according to an estimate from KP Law, the firm leading the action.

title: French Prosecutors Seek Charges Against Elon Musk's X Over Child Abuse Content
content: The Paris public prosecutor's office said Wednesday it has opened an investigation into X on charges including complicity in possessing and distributing child sexual abuse images and unlawfully collecting personal data.

title: West Bengal Government Formation LIVE: BJP Prepares To Take Oath Tomorrow
content: West Bengal Government Formation LIVE Updates: Prime Minister Narendra Modi, Union Home Minister Amit Shah, BJP president Nitin Nabin, along with several Union ministers, are expected to attend the swearing-in ceremony.

title: West Bengal government formation LIVE: Amit Shah arrives in Kolkata for BJP legislature party meet to elect new CM
content: The swearing-in ceremony of the State's first BJP government is scheduled to be held at Brigade Parade Ground on May 9

title: Bottom 50% of Nagaland households receive only 18% of total income, says report
content: Nagaland recorded a Gini coefficient of 0.46, a measure commonly used for income inequality, where 0 represents perfect equality and 1 indicates extreme inequality

title: Amit Shah arrives in Kolkata to attend BJP's legislature party meeting, swearing in of new govt
content: Mr. Shah was received at the Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose International Airport by BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari

title: T.N. Government formation LIVE: CPI, CPI(M) hold executive committee meetings in Chennai
content: MP Manickam Tagore claims Congress exited alliance after realising DMK’s intent to join hands with AIADMK ‘for power’ in Tamil Nadu

title: India pushes for inclusive migration, stronger anti-trafficking laws at United Nations General Assembly
content: Kirti Singh highlighted the various extensive initiatives undertaken by India, including the use of digital technologies, to support its nationals abroad

title: Bharat is sacred, bound together by a sense of oneness: PM Modi
content: The Prime Minister, in a signed article marking 75 years since the restored Somnath Temple was reopened to devotees, said the struggles and sacrifices of those who laid down their lives to protect the temple in Gujarat and those who rebuilt it time and again will never be forgotten

title: Naidu directs Collectors to clear APIIC lands from Section 22A list, push MSME sector
content: The Chief Minister tells the Registration Department to clear such lands through the Inspector-Generals concerned, and asks Collectors to resolve land allotment issues without delay

title: Lakhimpur Kheri case: Supreme Court expresses disappointment over non-production of witnesses
content: It asked the trial judge to make an endeavour to conclude the trial in a time-bound manner, and also to file a status report before it

title: Massive security grid with over 2,000 police personnel, sniper teams and 150 CCTV cameras at Parade Ground for PM Modi’s meeting
content: Metro stations and other elevated structures overlooking the Parade Ground may be screened off or covered

title: Seeking a new paradigm for Dravidian exceptionalism
content: At the heart of Vijay’s emphatic win, lies a disenchantment with the smug complacence of rule by both the DMK and, for a decade until 2021, the AIADMK

title: Delhi HC to pass interim order protecting Shashi Tharoor’s personality rights over deepfake videos
content: Justice Mini Pushkarna also issued notices to defendants in suit including social media platforms such as X and Meta, owner of Instagram

title: Kerala polls: Congress has democratic process for CM selection unlike BJP and CPI(M), says Sandeep Varier
content: Sandeep Varier, who was a BJP leader before joining Congress in 2024, contested on a Congress ticket in the recently concluded Assembly election in Kerala

title: India at Venice Biennale 2026 curtain raiser: Many voices, one resonance
content: From soil and thread to immersive myth and monumental sculpture, India’s Venice Biennale 2026 presence expands beyond the pavilion into a layered meditation on home and identity

title: MoS for External Affairs Kirti Vardhan Singh meets U.N. Chief Antonio Guterres
content: Kirti Vardhan Singh who is in Yew York for second International Migration Review Forum met the U.N. Secretary General at the headquarters

title: Koruvada farmers in Anakapalli seek correction of land records after Jagananna resurvey
content: Eight farmers say their lands in Survey No. 327/4 were wrongly recorded as inam lands despite holding pattas and cultivation records.

title: NCRB report: Crime against elders spikes, Bengaluru has second highest crimes against women
content: Bengaluru’s crime rate stood at 117 cases per lakh population, significantly higher than Mumbai’s 74.6 and Chennai’s 25.4.

title: Dead foetus kept in womb ‘for 22 hours’ in Maharashtra’s Beed; health dept. forms committee to probe
content: Family of the woman has alleged that the doctor attempted to hide the negligence and gain financial benefits

title: Delhi launches 15-day awareness drive for Census 2027
content: The campaign, running from May 7-21, aims to spread awareness about the ongoing phase one of Census 2027

title: Kerala health authorities sound caution over mosquito-borne diseases
content: Stress laid on consistent field surveillance and control measures as stagnant water and intermittent rains create breeding grounds for mosquitoes, increasing risk of diseases

title: APGDA urges Health Minister to appoint DME from six eligible candidates
content: One of the six Additional Directors of Medical Education can be considered for the post, the association says in its representation to Health Minister Satya Kumar Yadav.

title: Class 12 State Board exams: Tamil Nadu registers 95.2% pass rate
content: Erode district recorded the highest pass percentage of 98.8, while Sivaganga district stood second with 98.05% pass, as per the data

title: Kerala’s Samridhi@Kochi budget eatery considers price hike as LPG costs surge
content: For now, Samridhi is operating on a combination of cooking gas and firewood since the supply of commercial cylinders, which was disrupted following the West Asia conflict, has yet to be fully restored

title: In Maharashtra, three-language policy creates another flashpoint, avoided temporarily
content: Language groups continue to be aggressive, say won’t relent till the government scraps the rule and the exam

title: Fabricated LPG shortage narrative fuels black-market cylinder sales across Hyderabad
content: According to Deputy Commissioner of Police (Task Force) Vaibhav Gaikwad, the artificial LPG shortage narrative is being fabricated at the delivery level by some agency and delivery personnel

title: Gurugram gated societies not letting in enumerators; FIRs recommended against enumerators obstructing Census work
content: Census Act, 1948 has provision for three years in jail for those found neglecting or obstructing Census duties; Gurugram Census officer writes to RWAs directing entry of Census workers with ID, authorisation letters

title: Nida Khan, key accused in Nashik TCS case, arrested in Maharashtra
content: Arrest comes over 40 days after FIR was registered against her in a rape and sexual exploitation case

title: Explosions in Amritsar, Jalandhar part of BJP’s ‘Mission Punjab’: AAP MP Sanjay Singh
content: The AAP leader alleged the BJP wants to carry out blasts, disturb peace, spread hatred, and incite conflict in the State

title: Tamil Nadu Class 12 board exam results: How to check?
content: Students can access their Class 12 results on the official websites tnresults.nic.in and dge.tn.gov.in

title: J&K Lieutenant Governor given telecom control powers during public safety events
content: Using the powers, the Lieutenant Governor can block the transmission, allow interception, order decryption of messages, and suspend telecom services in the event of any public emergency or in the interest of public safety

title: At Sankat Mochan Sangeet Samaroh, artistes of all faiths mark their attendance at Hanuman’s durbar
content: The 103‑year‑old Sankat Mochan Sangeet Samaroh reflects Benaras’s inclusive spirit, where devotion and diverse musical traditions converge in Hanuman’s temple.

title: Centre calls for steps to safeguard shelter residents from heat
content: A communication from the Union Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs instructs authorities to ensure that shelters had functional fans, water dispensers, and other cooling facilities. It also calls for training shelter staff to identify signs of heat-related illnesses and implement response protocols

title: Mumbai family deaths: Rat poison ingredient zinc phosphide found in viscera and watermelon sample
content: Further probe is being conducted by the JJ Marg police station, which has recorded statements of kin and neighbours

title: Pre-SIR mapping picks up speed in Hyderabad, reaches 37%
content: More than 300 help desks have been established across the city, with at least 20 in each constituency, to assist voters in finding their constituency and polling station for the 2002 SIR

title: Satire | The Great Nicobar nuisance
content: Cut trees, save the nation: gifting the island to a dani can’t be worse than handing over the Indian Ocean to China

title: Faced harassment from a group of men onboard Delhi-bound flight, alleges MP Mahua Moitra
content: In social media post, she says the group ‘leered’ at her and their actions violate her safety in an aircraft; she tags airline and Minister in the post, says she will file formal complaint later

title: Tamil Nadu government formation: Options and precedents before Governor Arlekar
content: A former official of the Tamil Nadu government, who is well-versed with parliamentary practices and conventions, explains that there is nothing wrong with the Governor seeking letters of support, but he or she should not carry out a head count. The floor of the House is the best place for any party to demonstrate its strength

title: A year after Operation Sindoor, two schools bear the cost of conflict in Pulwama and Poonch
content: Schools in J&K damaged in Pakistani retaliation recall trauma caused to children following the deaths of students; one school awaits compensation to rebuild infrastructure

title: Watch: Vijay won, but why can’t he become Chief Minister yet?
content: For the first time since 1952, Tamil Nadu has delivered a fractured mandate in an Assembly election. The man behind this turn of events is cinema superstar Vijay, who not only entered politics but also stunned the two Dravidian rivals, the DMK and AIADMK. But Vijay's TVK managed only 108 seats on its own, short of the majority mark of 118. Vijay will need the support of smaller parties to form the government and with the Congress in the mix, it poses a very interesting situation. So, what are the scenarios? What role does the Governor play?

title: How does Kerala plan to tackle oil spill hazards?
content: What prompted the drafting of the Oil Spill Contingency Plan? How will it help protect Kerala’s coastline?

title: Pinarayi promises to listen to people, make corrections
content: He says the Left will be a responsible Opposition, supporting the new government’s actions to improve the standard of living of the people while opposing its anti-people policies

title: Guiding young minds to scale greater heights: The success of Andhra’s State-run schools
content: Reforms ushered in by the Andhra Pradesh government in State-run schools have led to a significant improvement in the recent Class 10 results, underscoring that committed investment, both in intent and resources, drives better outcomes, finds out P. Sujatha Varma

title: Caught between forests and fear: Karnataka’s growing human–animal conflict crisis
content: From Kodagu’s hills to Hassan’s farmlands, rising encounters with elephants, gaurs and big cats are claiming lives, exposing gaps in mitigation, and leaving residents demanding urgent, lasting solutions, find Darshan Devaiah B.P. and G.T. Sathish

title: Vehicles with fake number plates used to shoot Suvendu Adhikari’s aide: West Bengal police
content: Eyewitnesses say gunshots heard around 10 p.m.; seized car, getaway bike used fake plates, say police; alleging that this was a targeted assassination, Mr. Adhikari demands death penalty for the killers

title: Telangana’s 2BHK dreams caught between delays, distance and vacancies
content: Several beneficiaries of Telangana’s double-bedroom housing scheme continue to wait for their allotted homes as construction remains incomplete. For several low-income families, rising rents and cost of living have deepened financial stress, turning the dream of a permanent house into an urgent necessity. But in a contrasting trend, several allotted flats in Hyderabad remain unoccupied. What steps is the government taking to complete pending works and improve occupancy in the housing colonies? K. Shiva Shanker, P. Laxma Reddy and P. Sridhar uncover the ground realities

title: Caste-iron control: The threat of Rajasthan’s caste panchayats
content: Extrajudicial panchayats in Rajasthan use the age-old tool of social ostracisation combined with extortionary fines to control communities. Sreeparna Chakrabarty reports on how the Rajasthan High Court has taken note of this parallel justice system within each caste, the victims’ plight, and lack of a law that targets the social evil

title: Kerala election results: Congress high command to pick Kerala CM; most UDF allies for Satheesan
content: A three-way fight is on in Kerala for the Chief Minister’s chair; outgoing Leader of Opposition V.D. Satheesan, Alappuzha MP K.C. Venugopal, and senior leader Ramesh Chennithala are in the race

title: West Bengal Governor dissolves State Legislative Assembly
content: The 17th West Bengal Legislative Assembly which was sworn in after the 2021 assembly elections stands dissolved; swearing in of the first BJP government in West Bengal will be held at Brigade Parade Grounds on Saturday

title: CPI(M), Left front launch exercise to identify shortcomings, lapses; Pinarayi Vijayan likely to be Opposition leader in Kerala Assembly
content: Leaders say party forums at various levels will listen to their immediate lower units, down to the branch, the grassroots rung of the CPI(M), as part of an intense and self-critical process of examination

title: Akhilesh Yadav calls on Mamata Banerjee, says Trinamool fought well
content: Votes in the West Bengal Assembly poll had been stolen by the BJP, EC and other agencies, SP leader alleges; ‘If the right to vote is taken away, then what remains of democracy?” he says

title: Governor’s remit is to invite TVK chief Vijay and ask for a floor test: former Law Minister Ashwani Kumar
content: The due process argument attributed to the Governor of Tamil Nadu is seen as a graceless stratagem on the part of the Governor to delay Vijay’s swearing-in despite a deafening popular mandate in his favour, he says

title: DMK explores backing AIADMK, Tamil Nadu Governor tells Vijay he hasn’t established majority
content: The CPI(M), CPI and the VCK hold the key to any further churn in Tamil Nadu politics; in fact, their combined support is crucial for both the TVK and the AIADMK to form a government

title: West Bengal tops States in crimes by foreigners; Bangladeshis top accused: NCRB
content: The National Crime Records Bureau data showed that crimes against foreigners rose from 238 cases in 2023 to 257 in 2024

title: Ally Cong. seeks ‘better coordination over crucial public issues’ with ruling NC
content: “Mutual understanding and cooperation among coalition partners were non-negotiable for smooth governance and effective public welfare. The NC cannot afford to ignore the voice of its alliance partner,” said senior Congress leader and legislator Ghulam Ahmad Mir

title: Students of sealed seminary-cum-school hit streets, demand re-opening in Kashmir’s Shopian
content: Hundreds of students, accompanied by parents, hit the streets in south Kashmir and marched towards the office of the Deputy Commissioner

title: Congress blocks Agra-Mumbai highway in Indore over farmers’ issues
content: The demonstration, held in the Pigdambar area as part of the Congress’s State-wide ‘chakka jam andolan’, led to long queues of vehicles on both sides of the highway

title: Several drugs consignments recovered along Gujarat coast
content: Officials said personnel of the 176 Battalion of the BSF recovered ten packets of the contraband during a patrol operation in the creek region of Kutch on Wednesday

title: Twin blasts: Opposition accuses CM of ‘sedition’; BJP plotting unrest in State, says AAP
content: Jakhar-led team meets DGP, presses for probe into Mann’s statements on explosions, accuses him of giving ‘clean chit’ to Pakistan; Sanjay Singh says attacks part of BJP’s ‘Mission Punjab’
